item.title: Why60 MinutesShould Take Critiques of Its Work Seriously
item.description: Scott Pelley’s recent interview reveals why the show shouldn’t ignore the accusations of bias.
item.dc:creator: Conor Friedersdorf

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Cornball in Chief
item.description: The secretary of defense is a fountain of corny performances and rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols

item.title: The ‘Presumption of Regularity’ Is Evaporating
item.description: Judges have long defaulted to a posture of trust toward the federal government, but under Trump that is changing, and a new set of legal possibilities is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Quinta Jurecic

item.title: Hunter Biden’s Life After Shame
item.description: What is the former president’s son hoping to achieve?
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: As American as the Forward Pass
item.description: To understand the history of the nation, look to the history of its sports.
item.dc:creator: Sally Jenkins

item.title: The One Area Where a 14-Year-Old Is an Adult
item.description: Lawmakers are finding catharsis in sending children to prison.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Bruenig

item.title: American Christians Face a Choice
item.description: The faithful can still repair the wreckage they have wrought.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wehner

item.title: How Britain Became as Poor as Mississippi
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon

item.title: The Revolutionary Gordon Wood
item.description: The historian who recovered the radicalism of the American founding died on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Craig Bruce Smith

item.title: Actually, the SAT Was Necessary After All
item.description: University of California faculty are in open revolt over the lack of standardized-test scores.
item.dc:creator: Idrees Kahloon
